Project.name: Mole
Project.annotation: macOS system maintenance CLI with AI-powered advisor. Disk cleanup, optimization, health checks, uninstall management, and LLM-driven interactive system analysis with risk-tagged cleanup recommendations.
Project.keywords: macOS, system-maintenance, disk-cleanup, optimization, bash, cli, ai-advisor, llm-integration, openai-compatible, interactive-menu, safe-deletion

Actors.actor-User: macOS user running mole commands from Terminal. Interactive selection of AI recommendations.
Actors.actor-LLM: External OpenAI-compatible LLM endpoint. Receives system data, returns structured analysis and cleanup plan.
Actors.actor-System: macOS filesystem, system APIs (sysctl, vm_stat, df, du). Read-only data source for collector.

UC-001.Action: Run `mo advisor` to get AI-powered system analysis
UC-001.Goal: Understand what is consuming disk space and receive actionable cleanup recommendations
UC-001.Preconditions: AI endpoint configured via `mo advisor --setup`. Terminal with interactive TTY.
UC-001.AcceptanceCriteria: 1. System data is collected (disk, memory, CPU, caches, containers, build artifacts, Docker, Homebrew) 2. Data is sent to configured LLM endpoint 3. LLM returns structured report: disk usage summary, items not recommended for deletion, low-risk items, medium/high-risk items 4. Report is rendered as formatted terminal output 5. User sees interactive [x]/[ ] selection menu with all recommended actions 6. User confirms with typed "yes" 7. Selected items are deleted via safe_remove pipeline 8. Execution summary shows items removed, space recovered, items skipped

UC-002.Goal: Set up LLM connection (endpoint URL, model name, API key, parameters)
UC-002.AcceptanceCriteria: Configuration saved to ~/.config/mole/ai.conf. Values validated on input. Connection test optional.

UC-003.Action: Run `mo advisor --dry-run` to inspect collected data
UC-003.Goal: See what system data would be sent to the LLM without making an API call
UC-003.AcceptanceCriteria: All collector sections displayed. No API call made. No deletions proposed.

UC-004.Action: Use existing mole commands (clean, optimize, check, manage, uninstall)
UC-004.AcceptanceCriteria: Operations execute safely with protected-path validation, sudo when needed, user confirmation for destructive actions.

NonGoals.item-1: No background daemon or scheduled cleanup in v1.
NonGoals.item-2: No GUI — CLI and interactive terminal only.
NonGoals.item-3: No model training or fine-tuning — uses external LLM APIs as-is.
NonGoals.item-4: No automatic deletion without user confirmation — always requires interactive approval.
NonGoals.item-5: No system-level operations requiring SIP disable.

Constraints.constraint-1: Must run on macOS 13+ (Ventura and later), Apple Silicon and Intel.
Constraints.constraint-2: Bash only — no compiled dependencies, only standard macOS CLI tools + python3 for JSON parsing.
Constraints.constraint-3: API key must never appear in logs or terminal output.
Constraints.constraint-4: Protected paths (system directories, critical user data) must never be deleted, even if LLM recommends them.
Constraints.constraint-5: Must work with any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio, OpenRouter, etc.).

Risks.risk-1: LLM may recommend paths that do not exist or are protected. Executor must validate every path before deletion.
Risks.risk-2: du -sk on large/protected directories can hang indefinitely. Collector must use timeouts.
Risks.risk-3: set -e in sourced files (common.sh chain) can kill the advisor subprocess. Must be handled with set +e and subprocess isolation.
Risks.risk-4: LLM response format may vary between models. JSON extraction must be robust with fallbacks.
Risks.risk-5: SIP-protected directories (Library/Caches root, Application Support) return partial or zero sizes from du.

OpenQuestions.question-1: Should the advisor support multiple LLM profiles (e.g. fast-local model vs powerful-cloud model)?
OpenQuestions.question-2: Should the advisor remember previous recommendations and skip already-reviewed items?
OpenQuestions.question-3: Should there be a non-interactive mode (e.g. `mo advisor --auto-safe`) that auto-applies only SAFE items?
